one_line_description: Open-source (Magento Open Source) and commercial (Adobe Commerce) ecommerce platform with REST and GraphQL web APIs.
auth_summary: Self-hosted Magento/Adobe Commerce supports token-based auth (admin/customer bearer tokens) and admin-created 'Integrations' that generate consumer key/secret + access token pairs signed with OAuth 1.0a (not OAuth2). Adobe Commerce as a Cloud Service instead uses OAuth2 via Adobe IMS, including server-to-server flows.
access_notes: Anyone can run Magento Open Source for free and create integration/admin tokens in the admin panel self-serve; Adobe Commerce (paid product) works the same way per instance, while the newer Cloud Service requires an Adobe org and IMS-based OAuth2 credentials.
api_surface_summary: REST and GraphQL APIs cover catalog, carts/checkout, orders, customers, inventory, CMS, promotions, and admin configuration — several hundred REST routes.
agent_notes: Auth is fragmented across editions: legacy OAuth 1.0a integrations (not in the allowed enum; noted here), plain bearer admin tokens, and OAuth2/IMS only for the new Cloud Service. Like WooCommerce, connections are per-instance (user supplies their own base URL + token). Multiple community MCP servers exist (magento-mcp, magemcp, Codexpect, CData); no Adobe-official MCP found.
